title: EEG: Three armed bandit gambling task
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 23 healthy college students performing a three-armed bandit gambling task with dynamically oscillating reward probabilities. The task was designed to investigate neural correlates of reward processing and reward prediction error during adaptive decision-making and task set generation. Data were collected in 2014 at the University of New Mexico's Cognitive Rhythms and Computation Lab.
methods_description: EEG data were collected while subjects performed a three-armed bandit task with three stimulus options (blue, red, green) that oscillated between 20% and 90% reinforcement probability in different phases. Corrugator EMG and skin conductance measurements were also recorded on most participants; however, data quality for these measurements was limited and they were not extensively analyzed. Task implementation was programmed in MATLAB.
